ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE DISMISSAL PURSUANT TO CRC 3.1385 SET FOR APR-08-2014 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. JUN-19-2013 CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING STIPULATED JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF CIP AIRPORT INDUSTRIAL/FLEX PORTFOLIO LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT HAMBRECHT, WILLIAM R. $416,182.99 PRINCIPAL, $3,125.00 ATTORNEY FEES, $6,573.70 INTEREST, $450.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $426,331.69, SEE SCANNED DOC
EXECUTION FOR MONEY ISSUED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY AS TO DEFENDANT HAMBRECHT, WILLIAM R.

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED FILED BY PLAINTIFF CIP AIRPORT INDUSTRIAL/FLEX PORTFOLIO LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY AS TO THIRD PARTY SCHWEIZER, ANNA OEX HEARING SET FOR FEB-25-2014 AT 02:00 PM IN DEPT 514
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-15-2014 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF CIP AIRPORT INDUSTRIAL/FLEX PORTFOLIO LLC'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING JUDGMENT DEBTOR'S COMPLIANCE WITH SUBPOENA DUCES TECUM AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. CIP'S MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART. JUDGMENT DEBTOR SHALL PRODUCE ALL OF THE DOCUMENTS THAT ARE SUBJECT TO THE MOTION TO COMPEL, EXCEPT AS TO DEMAND 33 & 34, WHICH ARE DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. JUDGMENT DEBTOR MAY DESIGNATE DOCUMENTS AS CONFIDENTIAL PURSUANT OT A STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER THAT MEETS THE REQUIREMENT OF CRC 2.550 AND 2.551. THE STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER PROPOSED BY JUDGMENT DEBTOR IN FEDERAL PROCEEDINGS AND DOES NOT COMPORT WITH CALIFORNIA LAW. IT CREATES CONTRARY TO THE HOLDING OF NBC SUBSIDIARY (KNBC-TB),INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1999)20 CAL.4TH 1178, A BLANKET PRESUMPTION OF CONFIDENTIALITY WITHOUT THE SPECIFIC FINDINGS THAT CRC 2.550 REQUIRES THE COURT TO MAKE PRIOR TO ALLOWING EVIDENCE TO BE FILED UNDER SEAL. BOTH PARTIES' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDERS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM ERNEST A. HALPERIN, CLERK K. LIU, CSR RHIANNON SMITH.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Judgment Debtor'S Compliance With Subpoena Duces Tecum And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances, Atty. S. Judy Hirahara of Cadden & Fuller LLP (949-788-0827) appear for plaintiff CIP Airport Industrial/Flex Portfolio LLC moving party. / Atty. Dennis D. Miller of Stein & Lubin LLP (415-981-0550) appear for defendant William R. hambrecht, opposing party.

Hearing held, after parties met and conferred the court rules, PLAINTIFF CIP AIRPORT INDUSTRIAL/FLEX PORTFOLIO LLC's Motion For Order Compelling Judgment Debtor'S Compliance With Subpoena Duces Tecum And For Monetary Sanctions. CIP's motion is granted in part. Judgment debtor shall produce all of the documents that are subject to the motion to compel, except as to demand 33 & 34, which are denied without prejudice. Judgment debtor may designate documents as confidential pursuant ot a stipulated protective order that meets the requirement of CRC 2.550 and 2.551. The stipulated protective order proposed by judgment debtor in federal proceedings and does not comport with California law. It creates contrary to the holding of NBC subsidiary (KNBC-TB),Inc. v. Superior Court (1999)20 Cal.4th 1178, a blanket presumption of confidentiality without the specific findings that CRC 2.550 requires the court to make prior to allowing evidence to be filed under seal. Both parties' request for sanctions are denied. Orders signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem Ernest A. Halperin, Clerk K. Liu, CSR Rhiannon Smith (619-318-1225).
